stay confused and goy

62152134

62152257
>>62149991
>sees a White person
>seethes and talks about his penis

Serious question: why are brown/black/yellow people like this?

62152274
>>62150535
It's very easy to tell Yiddish names from actual German names if you know German. Jews were not allowed to learn crafts so they never have names like
>Schneider
>Müller
>Jäger
Instead, they usually combined words of nature to create surnames
>Rosenberg (rose mountain)
>Goldfarb (golden color)
>Grünspan (green chip)
The presence of -'stein' is no clear indicator either. Is it the name of an actual place like
>Regenstein
>Wallenstein
>Fürstenstein
? Then it's not a jew. If it's something like
>Goldstein
>Feinstein
it's clearly a jew.

62152340
CCI is what actually measures how main street is doing. How the fuck does trump still have plebe support at this point?

https://www.advisorperspectives.com/dshort/updates/2026/03/31/two-measures-of-consumer-attitudes-march-2026

62152354
you should be glad he's an actual german and not a kike.
>zero investing experience
see below
>doesn't even work in finance
see below again
>worked for SBF
here's the finance experience you claim doesn't exist. retard. he was smart enough to see the writing on the walls and got out while he still could
>fired from OpenAI for behavior unbecoming
so he pissed off a top kike? whats the problem here?
>given billions of dollars to play with just...because
so he pissed off a top kike? and went off and did his own thing with apparently great success? whats the problem here?

62152374
>>62152354
>What's the problem here
The problem is I want a billion dollars and do not want other people to have a billion dollars. This kid can get fucked, I should be getting prime teen pussy not this fucking zoomer faggot

62152385
>>62152257
My exes friend had a bf like this. He had the long horse face sunken eyes and a 12 inch penis. I had to dump my ex because she was constantly trying to pursue him. It got to the point where our mutual friend was asking me to tell her to stop hitting on him.

It was unreal man. This guy mogged me to death.

item.title: "100k a year" is a meme — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
"100k a year" is a meme

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:57 UTC

OP

The idea that 100k a year and "six figures" is a lot of money is leftover from the boomer era. If you make 100k, you pay 22k in fed income tax in the US. So now you have 78k. If you live in a state with state income tax, you pay more. But let's imagine you live in a good state to keep it simple. 78k a year is only 6.5k a month. A normie could very easily blow that and save nothing, especially if they have kids.

Early replies (first 3)

62152809
>>62152797
>78k
sir you haven't saved a penny for retirement, this is muttmerica not eurobongistan
try 58k for a reasonable contribution to a 401k that doesn't ensure you live destitute as a geriatric

62152815
>>62152797
a balding pederast made this thread

62152822
No shit, its why boomers are stuck thinking in 1990 levels of compensation. Like $25 a hour will provide you a liveable wage or $100k a year means you're fucking loaded.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152834
>>62152797
After saving max 401K, max HSA, and deductions for medical, life insurance, etc, I take home about $7K a paycheck on $380K gross.

62152839
>>62152797
>only 6.5k a month
I hate americans. 1.5k a month is liveable. 4k with a family. 5k for luxury.

62152852
>>62152839
t. didn't have breakfast today.

62152855
>>62152834
Real Worlder Moment

62152858
>>62152797
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62152870
>>62152834
op never paid fucking tax....look at how he calculated that shit. full amount at marginal and then didnt take out all the little bullshit or state taxes. this nigger never even had a paycheck in his life.

62152885
>>62152858
You're not a millionaire if you can't cash out. And nearly all of these fucks are house-rich

62152940
>>62152797
no shit. its like making 50k or less in 2015

62152945
>you work for 25/hr or 52k a year
>make about 1500 a month on regular scalp trades, 18k a year
>this is 70k
>you get a second job 20 hours on friday/saturday/sunday
>this is another 26k
>96k
>you make one good biggish trade and make 20k on that
>sell 5000 in goods on ebay
>121k
What's really offensive is how hard it is to make over 100k and how much they rape you on it.

62153202
>>62152797
man how hard u get fucked over by taxes the more you make is kinda wild.. obviously the extremely rich dont face this problem but the 200k-400k range u get fucked so hard

62153768
>>62152797
So what, you cucks worrying about the value of 100k don't even deserve to be making that much to begin with. No need to be so concerned about 100k if you can't even make 50k

OP

You don't keep your coins stored on exchanges, right /biz/?

https://www.polskieradio.pl/395/7786/Artykul/3678256,head-of-polish-crypto-exchange-flees-to-israel-amid-fraud-investigation

Early replies (first 3)

62152161
>>62152123

I don't. People who do deserve to lose it.

62152171
>>62152123
surely israel will act like a reasonable civilized nation and return the thief to face justice

62152188
>>62152171
Israel does not extradite its citizens.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152216

Its in their "holy bible" that we are just cattle for them, ofc they will extradite the blanked dick guy....

>>62152216

Idk about these things, i am not religious.

62152412

62152475

I mean besides big H memery, if you avoided all jeet shit (restaking protocols), and hebrew nonsense (FTX, Bancor, Celsius...), you avoided many suicide inducing events.
Funny that.

62152496

>>62152244
You’re an idiot who has never read the Bible.

62152510

HAHHAHAHA

62152527
look at his fucking face, they are truly subhuman

62152535
>>62152171

>>62152123

Are we Indians of Europe?

62153718

In Jewish culture he will be hailed the likes of how the Roman’s would honour a great military commander. Bubba Ray Dudley stole yo crypto nigga

62153729

item.content:encoded:
/GRT/ - The Graph

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:40 UTC

OP

Get your cheapies!

Come slurp your cheapies!

Come get'em before they're expensivies!

Early replies (first 3)

62145844
>>62145764
I LOOOOOVE me some GRT. Stack up on BAT and PNK while you're at it home boys.


You stupid nigger

62145884
>>62145844
In a few years GRT will be among the top 10 by mcap and you will remember me

62146164
>>62145764
Which is the most cucked coin? GRT, ROSE or ICP? I hold all three.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146573
>>62146164
Depends on your entry prices
GRT is a great hold if you buy at this point, since it won't go much lower
Do tell me, how long have you been bagholding?

62149998
>>62146573
My first buy was a 10k suistack in January 2021 I think. Around 30 cents. I proceeded to roundtrip the whole $2+ pump back down and never sold. I topped up to 50k GURTs like 2 years ago or something so my average is 10 cents now. I've been buying all the other cucked coins but not GURT because the tokenomics are unironically shit and while I'm sure GURT isn't going away from an adoption perspective, Yanniv Tranniv stole the query fees in the end and also Tegan is a retarded jewish cunt, which should be bullish if not for the fact she is a roastie. It's over.

62150018
>>62145764
I would, but….It’s Over

62150135
>>62150018
Haven't you heard?

It's not over anymore, that part is what's over.

62150157
>>62149998
>Tegan is a retarded jewish cunt

I was gonna say we should milk her publicly and that it should improve GRT's price action

But I checked up on her and it seems she is battling cancer

This is bad bros, the project needs someone who can be publicly milked or the price will stay in the gutter

62150162
>>62145764
Turns out Tegan has cancer bros...
It looks pretty bad
>Not like this

62150252
>>62150157
>>62150162
>It's real
Damn now I feel like a cunt. Maybe this is God's punishment for letting Yanniv Tranniv steal the query fees and prevent all of us from making it thoughever.

62150822
>>62150252
>Maybe this is God's punishment for letting Yanniv Tranniv steal the query fees and prevent all of us from making it thoughever
lmao you're a cunt

62151077
>>62145764
Here's a poem about how Yaniv the Traniv stole all the query fees and doomed the project:

Yaniv the Traniv slid into the code,
Tiptoed light on the data road,
Indexers grinding, “profits ahead!”
Yaniv said, “Nice,” and siphoned instead.

Delegators dreaming of APY highs,
Yaniv bought lunch with their staking supplies,
Curators yelling, “This graph will boom!”
Yaniv took fees and rented a room.

Where did the query fees go? Who knows?
Vanished like socks or your last pesos,
Dashboards green, everything “fine,”
Yaniv just whispered, “All mine, all mine.”

He skimmed each query, quick and neat,
Turned every click into something to eat,
dApps kept pinging, none the wiser,
Yaniv leveled up as a “fee optimizer.”

Then came a day when the math felt off,
APR coughing a digital cough,
They traced the trail—too late to believe—
Signed at the bottom: “Yaniv the Traniv — GG, I leave.”

62151146
>>62145764
Nigger I bought this shit at like a dollar 1.60

62151160
>>62151146
In that case it's the perfect moment to buy more
Ya' know, dollar-cost averaging and stuff

62152752
>>62150822
Checked
>>62151077
Kek. Obvious AI slop but nice dubs

62153106
I'm the GUURRRRRRRRTTTTT MAAAAAAAAAAAN

62153687
>>62145764
I LOVE me some yoGRT!

item.title: Python for finance/trading work — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Python for finance/trading work

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-04-24 20:07 UTC

OP

A ton of the finance/trading job postings I come across want a candidate knowledgeable with python/SQL. Ideally, I would love to focus on "strategic opportunities" aka asymmetric risk trades based on theories, information that is not well known or understood, etc. as a researcher/analyst/trader. But even "Research Associate" jobs want coding experience. Any kind anons have any resources that helped them learn python for this sort of purpose? Also what even is the point with AI coding advancements?

Early replies (first 3)

62149638
>>62149632
Oh yeah and also I want to work at a crypto-specific firm instead of tradfi if that matters... any suggestions on a python project to impress people in interviews/a resume are very appreciated also

62153602
>>62149632

maybe take some udemy courses or free youtube videos. probably can't find classes specifically designed for that. unless you find a private tutor or teacher and tell him that. he may or may not have the experience and might charge you up the ass.

i'm also interested in learning python and i'm trying to get an in-person class to motivate me more but they seem to be rare or very expensive. i tried one of those online bootcamps years ago for programming and i couldn't stay motivated and they moved too fast for my liking.

62153705
>>62149632
you have literal AI platforms you can prompt them as teachers for those specific coding niches and youre on here asking others how to find resources to learn? Holy shit anon you are the NPCs they talk about
>NGMI
my recommendation: go into construction, if you haven't learned to ask AI for research yet you definitely wont get a job as what youre asking for. This is the era of open intelligence and you seem blind to it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62153765
>>62149632
you can't just say you can do it unless you're fully retarded and still unable to figure things out even with an LLM giving you all the answers and practically writing everything for you?

The bar has definitely moved for fuck's sake THIS is why Indians will take your job

62153817
>>62149632
>>62153602

1. You guys are making this way more complicated than it needs to be. You can't write "Select all fields from the stocks table where the price is under 2 dollars" or "SELECT * FROM stocks WHERE price < 2"?

Python is literally the same shit:
if OP:
OP.sexual_preference = fag

That's literally it, you can learn all of this in like a week max, I am not even kidding. ChatGPT can teach you to write basic SQL fucking TODAY. Biggest hurdle is finding a test databse and understanding the high level concepts. Pick any fucking course.

2. Something, something AI - you still have to understand what SQL and Python are, but yea, grinding leet code problems is a waste at this point.

3. You conflated 2 high level skills - "muh assymetric risk trades" - did you go to a target school? Do you have CFA? Basically these jobs don't exist.

AMA, CFA & Software Architect before I lost all my money and moved in with my grandma. I have less than $200 across all my assets, including the $5 from the tendies on my desk.

>>62153705
>>62153765
These fucks aren't trolling. But it's also why AI alone won't fully solve things. OP can't prompt

item.title: Cathie Wood says t he bottom is not in: Bitcoin closed Q1 near $68,200 after breaking through three major on-chain suppo… — 6 replies — 14.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cathie Wood says t he bottom is not in: Bitcoin closed Q1 near $68,200 after breaking through three major on-chain suppo…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:43 UTC

OP

Cathie Wood says t he bottom is not in:

Bitcoin closed Q1 near $68,200 after breaking through three major on-chain supports. The 200-day moving average at $90,613, the short-term holder cost basis at $82,767, and the on-chain mean at $78,039 all gave way.

In addition, the supply in profit compressed from 78% to 50% before recovering. However, it never crossed below the supply in loss. That threshold typically signals a deep-value capitulation.

ARK places the true downside zone between the realized price near $54,000 and the investor price near $50,000. A break into that range has historically flagged a high-conviction global bottom, but neither level was breached in Q1.

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/bitcoin-whales-accumulate-69-more-052711529.html

Early replies (first 3)

62152284
ARK's data shows supply held by conviction buyers jumped from 2.13 million BTC to 3.60 million BTC in three months. The 69% increase marks the fastest absorption phase since the 2020 cycle.

That buying into a sharp price decline mirrors historical patterns where long-term holders absorb panic selling. ARK describes the pace as evidence that strong-handed participants viewed the drawdown as an opportunity.

Spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) balances reinforce the view, closing March near 1.29 million BTC and staying roughly flat quarter over quarter.

“Such stability suggests that institutional conviction remains strong, even amid significant downside volatility,” the report read.

62152293
>>62152282
Yeah bro open up your shorts, some boomer said its going to $50000 because thats what it did 4 years ago

62152298
>>62152293
I'm not personally opening any shorts, just sharing info. I have no idea what's going to happen

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152359
indicators are for midwits

62153382
sorry gents, cathie use to have good picks but my dick gave her brain damage

62153702
>>62153382
>subtle scornful remark since anon lost top dick pick status
Its time to move on
She's not even hot

item.title: How do I check the state of oil reserves per country? — 7 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I check the state of oil reserves per country?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:33 UTC

OP

Google search only shows oil reserves from a month ago or annual change over many years. I need data that shows the state of oil reserves day by day for a few key countries and political blocks: Japan, South Korea, EU, India and Australia are particularly interesting.

Early replies (first 3)

62149589
>>62148365
bump

62149601
>>62148365
Reserves don't change day by day by any meaningful amount. I would start by learning what oil reserves are.

62149830
>>62148365
I think this number is not published.

In many cases there is no single "oil reserve. Total oil reserves can include both state reserves and commercial reserves of individual refineries, ports, etc. A commercial reserve can include both legally mandated reserves and voluntary reserves for normal operations. Not everyone is required to report all of this, especially not on a day-to-day basis.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62149874
>>62148365
this is what claude said

62149876
>>62148365
Give them a call and ask

62152029
>>62149874
OP means oil in storage you fucking moron, not oil underground.

62153667
>>62152029
underground is storage, surfacelet.

item.title: I work for a startup company, been here for a couple years. — 3 replies — 10.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I work for a startup company, been here for a couple years.

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:51 UTC

OP

I work for a startup company, been here for a couple years. My boss has initiated a new “investment opportunity “ for staff only. Basically we would front him money for materials that would get turned into product that we sell. Just because we’ve been having great success & ramping up with B2B sales, and more inventory is needed to scale I guess. NOW WOULD IT BE RETARDED to get a 50k Amex loan at 8.99 APR to throw into this situation & reinvest the earnings. Bossman basically said we would make 10%-20% a month. If we ever want to pull out money out after material is bought but before it’s made, it’s ok. He will refund us what we put in. I know a coworker who has put in $66,000 and made $3700 in the first month.

Early replies (first 3)

62153666
time for a psychology lesson. a boss even suggesting the idea of using employees' personal wallets as business leverage shows an extremely aggressive risk-taking mindset. you'll need to be the judge of whether you think he's trying to share the wealth with the best intentions, or is he trying to position himself to reap the lion's share of the rewards while putting in the least amount of personal risk?

62153732
>>62153631
Why can't he take a business loan if the shit is so successful?

62153744
>>62153666
somewhat correct, satan,
but also - the team taking in some shares is pretty normal

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: PE is fundamentally broken and only serve to rob businesses of profit while saddling debt onto its owners. — 4 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
PE is fundamentally broken and only serve to rob businesses of profit while saddling debt onto its owners.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:30 UTC

OP

PE is fundamentally broken and only serve to rob businesses of profit while saddling debt onto its owners. LBOs at least contained the contamination of failing assets but now NAV debt has opened up the entire food chain as collateral. This leads to extended periods of time where exit is not available for these firms leading them to passing off the repayment to consumer prices. AI has already seen this happen with the experimentation of locking new users out of old price models in favor of doubled prices for the same product.

Early replies (first 3)

62149742
I love watching goycattle try to figure out how things work.

62149753
>>62149742
Me when I work at BlackRock and buy your grandma’s nursing home just to fire the nursing staff and let your grandma fend for herself as I restructure our annual expenditures to cut off grandma’s gas and electricity. That way I can take my exit package and resell the original business back to its owner with all the included debt I’ve accumulated in the restructuring.

62149760
>>62149753
Oh don’t forget I kill grandma with a gun and then turn Shady Oaks retirement into a data center because that’s the most efficient use of the real estate asset.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62153657
im sure thats nice and all (didnt read past 3 words) but have you considered nobody cares, this is jew daycare?

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 53 replies — 55.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:11 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146231
>>62145154
Stay lowkey

62146326
>>62146229
You can also stake your BTC if you want to earn from it, so it’s not just sitting idle without generating any yield.

62146497
>>62146326
Is it even safe to do that

62146523
>>62145244
Lmao

62146529
>>62146497
Defi protocols this year are brutal so why still choose this shit

62146577
>>62145154
People will expect you to be Santa everyday

62146661
>>62145144
Is it expensive?

62146671
>>62146529
Better safe than sorry goys

62147207
>>62146497
Yeah it’s safe since you stay in self custody while staking BTC for yield. Look into Babylon if you want to see how it actually works.

62147680
>>62146661
I think the jacket was $1000 lel

62148933
>>62146529
Yeah it’s been brutal, but not all protocols are like that. Bitcoin itself is secured by a strong base layer design. It’s not about chasing yield, it’s about using BTC without breaking its core security or custody model.

62148936
Never tell anyone how much money you have. Poor, rich, anything.

62148947
>>62141874
What makes you think that?

62149370
>>62146661
Like >>62147680 it's was around $1000

62149399
>>62142226
Did you enjoy losing 3 mil, fucker?

62149941
>>62138453
Only my brother knows I bought BTC, we both stake together now as well, with native staking gaining traction. Imagine if I told my parents to buy at 120K Kek.

62150719
>>62142197
You never know

62150903
>>62148933
Still it has risks

62151073
>>62146671
I get that, but it’s better to check first before saying that. So far, it fits my standards. I don’t think firms like Ledger or even A16z would back it otherwise.

62151201
>>62148936
Greedy bastard

62151260
>>62151073
it's the same thing. Everything will fail in the end. Nothing survive

62151310
NEVER

62151368
>>62149941
Heartaches

62151461
>>62138453
How do we confess them?

62151818
>>62149941
Nice are you also waiting for the TBV launch? More interesting for me since it could unlock more use cases.

62152719
>>62142226
That's the goal lol

62152732
never ever tell anyone you own any crypto holy shit that is like rule #1 in life don't tell me you idiots are trying to orange pill your fucking friends and family

62152763
>>62138453
Why would you ever brag about your money? Especially when every cryptobro is a scrawny geek. Do you want to get tortured by niggers for your private key?

Secret wealthy is better

62153574
>>62150903
Every system has risk. The difference is just what kind of risk you’re exposed to and how much control you still keep over your assets.

62153589
>>62152732
Maybe it depends on who you’re closest to in your family. There’s usually one person you really vibe with.

item.title: May need to declare bankrupcy due to severe schizo symptoms, advice? — 36 replies — 40.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
May need to declare bankrupcy due to severe schizo symptoms, advice?

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:15 UTC

OP

I'm worried about losing my pets and gaming pc and gaming console, it's all I have left. Does anyone know about bankruptcy or has anyone filed bankrupcy? I need advice I don't want to pay a lawyer yet or talk to a lawyer yet especially I don't want to tell a lawyer I have pets and a gaming PC and a console.

I need to file chapter 7 bankruptcy in California because I had what doctors call a schizophrenic episode where I got visions of what I thought was God talking to me and I destroyed all my property after I maxxed out my credit cards and destroyed everything almost (including old gaming PC) and changed all the passwords on my accounts. I haven't even signed into my bank account online thing in 4+ years and haven't used my debit card in 4 years. I experienced incarceration during this time and 4 years of homelessness.

I have 2 lizards lizard worth 200-600 dollars and a old gaming PC with hand me down parts and a Nintendo switch 2. And SSI (Schizobux) money.

I don't want to lose my pets or my gaming pc or my switch 2. I am on SSI and I think the wages cannot be garnished

To keep what I have left do I just ignore the debt collectors, let them sue me and ignore them because my only income can't be garnished? I'd rather go to jail than lose my pets and my steam account and my switch 2.

It's unlikely I will get a job through what doctors call severe schizo hallucinations and delusions to pay this stuff back. The longest I've worked consistently before through the hallucination stuff is 3-4 months then quit because it got too distressing to deal with.

I can't make the minimum payments with SSI. So I need bankruptcy, my family also can't afford to pay me. I was completely non-functional schizo (didn't brush my teeth and lived in the woods or at the beach homeless for 4 years) and my life got ruined, I think what is wrong with me is a spirit fuckin with me not schizo but I can't bring up demons or spirits in court.

Early replies (first 3)

62148662
The libtards over on reddit are saying it's your choice, but if you have the energy and time filing for bankruptcy sounds like it will leave your credit cleaner after the 7 year period. The libtards are also saying there's probably free legal help you can get. Without being a lawyer I can imagine if its unregistered and relatively low value like your games and pets it's pretty unfeasible it can in any way be repossessed. Just don't declare it anywhere, it's not like expensive artwork or collectibles.

62148665
>>62148566
I am god. Listen to my advice. I have not forsaken you, my son.
You need to piss into a cup and drink it, immediately.

62148725
>>62148662
What the difference in just letting the debt go to court and declaring bankrupcy? Excuse my TLDR I don't want to vent to people IRL about this except my mom.

I don't even want to risk my pets and gaming stuff being taken because I know it's technically property even if my pets are family to me and my gaming stuff is some of the only happiness I have right now.

I read it's unlikely they'll take pets or gaming stuff but possible. That's a risk.

Working is only possible with a mananger who is tolerant of potential constant small mistakes and odd behavior if I can push through literal rape hallucinations and deities shapeshifting into the store to test me which happened before. The voices said to ask my manager if i was literally in Hell and said the voices were an angel so I felt i was supposed to even though it'd be humiliating but instead I just said mental illness symptomsand quit.

I don't want to work through what I literally confused as genuine Hell before due to angel voices. Also I'd lose medical insurance if I worked, happened before they took away my SSI due to working part time but lukcily I got it back because I ended up homeless after quitting the job.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62148756
>>62148725
nobody wants to pawn your shit.
if you're serious about an heroing, offload as much of your stuff yourself as you can. it'll actually uplift you by keeping you busy. any money you can make feels like a tiny bit of progress. if you have any medical insurance left, try to get your meds straightened out. I felt so much better when they fixed my ADHD meds, suddenly everything was much easier. I'm still broke and going to the end but it makes the daily grind tolerable. Don't forget rollover IRAs and 401ks if you have them to put a loved one down as your beneficiary if you want to give that money. I did that but maybe take the 10% penalty if I feel like staying longer we'll see how the next fake interview goes

62148866
>>62148566
You need to start living below your means now and saving whatever you can. At least you got the schizobucks as a safety net, many people would kill to have even that. Put whatever you can save into Chainlink or something. You can survive on rice and beans for very little money if you buy them in bulk and with very little time investment if you cook them in bulk. Bean and rice burritos are goated poverty food. You can invest into good spices and some veggies like cilantro. Splurge on meat good cheese occasionally. But you will never make it if you don't get your mental sorted. As soon as you get your finances stable, you need to focus on mental health. If meds work for you, you need to be compliant w/ them; the risk is too high that you have another episode and burn everything down again. If your debts are way to high (like multiple years of income) I agree: fuck it, dont pay em. I know nothing about the bankruptcy process though desu. Also rec watching Eastern Orthodox videos on youtube and asking God to help you. GL anon.

62148899
>>62148866
>I agree: fuck it, dont pay em. I know nothing about the bankruptcy process though desu. Also rec watching Eastern Orthodox videos on youtube and asking God to help you. GL anon.
Anon you need to take your meds, too.

62148984
I am on meds and the only difference I notice on them is I don't have angry outbursts of behavior but hallucinations don't go away. I'm not willing to try more different meds, tried 7 already and those attempts made me obese. It took homelesness off meds to shed the weight, getting back on a low weight gaining med still made me 20 lbs overweight but its better than 80 lbs overweight.

The meds not working supports my belief it's a real spirit attacking me in my mind. I know in my heart its a real spirit but

I'll look into a lawyer maybe because of the exemption. I don't have over 10k in property.

I have

>Pets and pet enclosures
>Bed
>TV
>TV Stand
>Computer Desk
>old gaming PC that can play games on medium to high at only 60 FPS
>nintendo switch 2
>nintendo 3ds
>destroyed all physical games when hallucinating and only have digital games now
>like 12 cheap hanes t shirts, 5 pairs of amazon budget denim jeans and 2 jackets

I cant drive safely and dont even own a car. I live with my mom and she only charges me 300 a month in rent or i'd be homeless, i dont even have access to my bank account or credit card accounts because I changed the passwords when experiencing an episode and dont remember them or even what credit cards I had. waiting for collections letters to come in the mail.

Who knows about the exception in detail. Is it locked down legally I get to keep some property? Like,a guarantee legally?

I was 6k -8k in debt and I think with interest over the 4 years I was too unfunctional to even brush my teeth it went up substantially. I'd imagine it could be in the 10-20k range now but I dont know.


TLDR: Does anyone know in detail about the excemption for property in california when declaring bankrupcy?

62149203
cant tell if this is a troll, but ill bite bc im laughing so hard it hurts.

no one is taking (or wants) your pets bro. also consumer electronics are nothing. honestly, if I were you id apply for any line of credit I could. seriously just try anything and see if they give you any purchasing power. even if its only a few hundred go buy silver coins on credit and don't pay it back.

lmao at your lizards.

62149245
>>62149203
Why do you think I am trolling? I ruined my life during a spiritual attack doctors call schizophrenic episode. That's what happens during spiritual attack or schizophrenic episode, bizzare forms of life ruining.

They may not want my lizards but creditors are greedy pieces of shit potentially that don't give a shit about what I care about and love and just want money. They may not give two shits about taking my family members (pets) to get their money. I don't think I'm being paranoid.

62149284
>>62148899
Anon did not say how much debt he has or how much he makes on schizobucks. Obviously he should try to haggle down and pay it if off if possible.

62149331
>>62149245
Think about this for a second spaz. Bankruptcy procedures are typically used for businesses or people with complicated net worth & assets. Declaring personal bankruptcy is small beans compared to how it normally gets used. No assessor is coming to take inventory of your blender and lawnmower you fucking dork

62149373
>>62149284
I was around 6k in debt from memory but I dont know what it went up to with interest, I didn't check it for 4 years when completely disabled to the point i was homeless avoiding the voices commands.

I get 1260 or so with schizobux. 300 goes to rent, 120 to internet and phone. Then I spend like 80-100 on food a week.

That leave 840 for food and other expenses. 400-500 a month on food depending on if it's a 4 or 5 week month. Then I have 340 a month for anything else including taking care of pets. I also smoke cigarettes.

I'm thinking bankrupcy will be preferable to not smoking if i dont lose my pets and gaming pc and gaming console and TV

I'd literally have to have nothing at all for 7-10 years to pay it back on the minimum payment if they allowed 200-300 a month in payments total.

Bankrupcy seems better if i get to keep pets gaming console gaming pc and TV, because I'm just going to give my pets and shit to a relative or friend if they come for them. They arent getting my pets

62149381
>>62149331
I think creditors are greedy and may try to get every fucking penny personally, I don't know if they will for sure but it seems like a real risk to me

62149458
>>62149373
Well hopefully you can find some simple job that can supplement the schizo bucks, if they allow that. Don't smoke anon; you know how expensive nicotine addiction is. it just weakens your self control and is bad for mental health. I quit by switching to Zyns for some time, which i found much easier to quit than cigs, but still hard. Like i said, i don't know jack shit about bankruptcy but it does seem like the play if you would need to essentially live through 7+ years of slavery to pay it off. It doesn't seem like you are in a position to be doing any big borrowing anytime soon anyway, which is the only downside, no?

62149475
>>62148566

File for disability.

62149489
>>62149475
It's not enough to pay back debts, it's not even enough to pay for more rent than the generous $300 a month bill my mom gives me. I get 1260 or so a month for formal schizophrenia.

62149496
>>62149458
The symptoms make working even a simple job hard. It's hallucinations of rape which feels like real touch or a real spirit raping me.

But yeah 7+ years of slavery while working through rape hallucination type stuff doesn't seem worth it to not declare bankruptcy.That path seems like utter pain. Not going to do it. I think credit is ruined for 6-7 years from bankrupcy or something. Not sure.

62149502
>>62149496
>>62149489
You got enough money, fuck off richfaggot schizo quit begging.
Go sell your plasma.

62149515
>>62149496
I know you said that you don't want to change your meds around, but i don't think you can ever live a happy life having rape hallucinations, that is not okay bro. IDC if you have to go on a restrictive diet, you need to get your mental health addressed or else you NGMI even if you were rich. I would imagine even obesity is better than rape hallucinations.

62149516
>>62149502
I'm not begging anyone for fucking money. I was talking about bankruptcy.

62149532
>>62149516
Fuck off you stupid indian faggot I know who you are, post your hands now

62149774
>get fat
>blame meds
No dude, just count your calories. Take your meds.

62149783
>>62148566
Nigga Just bury your gaming pc before declaring bankruptcy. No one's gonna confiscate your lizards and sell them for $200

62149791
>>62149774
Clearly when I take the meds and eat 2 healthy meals a day I am 220ish pounds at 5 7 but when I'm homeless off meds and eating Jack in the Box 2-3 times a day I go down to 150 lbs or so, my normal weight not working out it's not the meds. The meds cause weight gain. Liars like you and doctors lying to my face through their fucking teeth to encourage me to take my meds, makes me stop taking them. The fucking paper from the fucking company says the meds cause weight gain. Lying to people with what doctors call schizophrenia to encourage or manipulate us into doing what you want us to do is what contributes to us not taking our meds. You don't know what's best for us.

I already fucking take meds but no on believes me because I still have rape hallucinations. It says online 25-50% of people don't respond to medicines if it's schizo. This makes it either a real spirit or medicine resistant psychosis from schizophrenia where i cannot tell it's schizophrenia because in my heart i believe it's a real spirit fucking with me genuinly.

62149795
>>62149791
FUCK OFF FAGGOT SCAMMER sitting waiting to respond to his bullshit samefagged thread
Go eat your lizards you pajeet begger.

62149800
Schizophrenia is just a symptom of having to deal in the real world. Goycattle can't take the strain.

62149809
>>62149791
The meds cant defy the laws of physics anon, your body can't magically spend more energy than it can take in because of a pill. If you eat a 1500 cal diet but burn 2000 cal a day, there is no possible way to be gaining weight, unless its just water weight.

62149814
>>62149809
you're talking to a serial beggar who spends all day making these gay "i need help but won't directly ask it"
Threads
He spent an hour and a half waiting for someone to bump his thread and immediately responded.

62149852
>Commiefornia
>have schizophrenia
Do you guys think theres something in the water or like air that makes californians go insane? I hear about more nutjobs in that state than anywhere else.

62149859
>>62149852
its the organic pesticides.

62149863
>>62148866
Vile Shlomo.

62151250
>>62149852
every mentally ill person in america moves there because the state guarantees free gibs, you also have them in florida, new york, washington

62153608
>>62148566
>Go on youtube
>Look up Jerry Marzinsky
>Watch his videos
>Stop being schizo

item.title: Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe… — 51 replies — 29.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe…

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-04-24 18:24 UTC

OP

Today, some guy said to my friend
>you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky
My friend goes
>wtf does that even mean? How come your parents aren’t rich? Are your parents stupid? You make it seem like being rich is based on luck and not intelligence. Does that mean your parents aren’t stupid AND unlucky?
I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”
Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
I noticed the bottom 90% pretend it was just luckto make themselves feel better.
Like come on, even your parents aren’t not rich which means you come from bad stock.
Like okay, sure, “daddy’s money.” How come your dad is poor?
Thoughts, /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62144840
>median HOUSEHOLD income
Why do they lie so much
A 22 year old in 1985 earning 20k is objectively different from a 22yo in 2023 earning 45k.

And median household income, at a time when people got married earlier is completely different from the median now where people get married later. So 22yos are actually even poorer, usually starting around 30k.

They lie so much it's no wonder people can't afford shit.

62144845
>>62144825
I swear people who say “daddy’s money” are poor. Like lets say someone is rich and has kids, would that person ever say “daddy’s money?”
It would not even make sense.
Like “mom’s basement” is another insult working class brokies use.

62144850
>>62144801
vote for open borders to add more illegals to compete with for housing and jobs. illegals obviously dont hurt the working class in any way ;)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62146466
>>62144801
TRVTH NVKE

62146615
>>62144801
>Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
Yeah sure but how does it benefit me?
Trump's son having "earned his money" with tips about oil mooning doesn't benefit me

62146619
>>62145534
How does what you're saying benefit him?
If you're gonna be useless then there's no reason for you to say anything. If you have nothing to say then shut up.

62146960
>>62146619
>OP asks for advice
>OP gets advice
>(you) nooooo you can't do that this isn't /adv/
OP's not gonna fuck you no matter how hard you simp for him, faggot.

62146991
I don't know OP, there is a saying "he was born on 3rd base so he thinks he hit a home run".

62147007
>>62144976
yes, its generally by poor brown people. They are as obsessed with counting other peoples money as kikes are.

62147036
the average American makes 115k after taxes. the average Amercian houshold takes home 250k.

62147062
>>62146960
>>OP gets advice
He's probably still waiting

62148071
>>62144801
My family isn't poor but not rich either. I make a good wagie wage of $150k/yr in my late 30s, comparable to what my dad made adjusted for inflation, but I am still a wagie. It isn't luck. Some of it is environment, some of it may even be genetics, but assuming your parents had you and raised you intentionally it's due to their actions and decisions that you are where you are and subsequently your grandparents and so on.

I did not receive any direct financial help from my parents apart from them giving me a good classical education that was homeschool when I was little and then a private high school. That is an advantage over just sticking your kids in public school as it meant I was able to have my college education mostly paid for with grants and did much better than average there which lead to good internships and a jump start on a career.

I also have parents who stayed together their whole lives. They provide a safety net I can fall back on temporarily if things go a little wrong without having to worry about being financially rekt. While I haven’t made use of this personally a couple of my siblings have and stayed with them while getting back on their feet. What this has done for me personally however is enable me to take some risks to increase my own position without worry. I'm also involved in my local church community thanks to my parents which is where I found my wife, where I found other job opportunities, and even smaller things like just being able to get a lot of items like furniture, baby stuff, etc for free, having people to split a whole cow for beef with, baby sitters, piano lessons for the kids, stuff like that. This is a type of wealth that is unquantifiable but has a marked impact on your ability to thrive and progress. So even though I didn't get a free million dollars from my parents or anything like that, the way they raised me and the environment they put me in set me up to also be solidly middle class.

62148084
>>62148071
Genetically speaking there is spousal choice, another kind of unquantifiable factor that affects not just intelligence, but also physical capabilities, looks, etc. It's not random, it's not luck, it's a choice.

I am doing the same things for my kids. They may not become ultra wealthies, but I can at least give them every advantage in becoming little chads and making it to the upper half of wagies.

62148207
>>62144801
Technically they didn’t earn but that doesn’t mean the aren’t entitled to it.
My kids will get a healthy inheritance, I think that’s one of the responsibilities of parenthood.

62148298
>>62144801
BRING OUT THE GUILLOTINE

62148321
>THATS BECAUSE POVERTY IS A CHOICE

62148369
>>62144801
Sometimes its skill, sometimes its luck, most often its a combination of both. And on top of that you need to be driven by ambition (ie. have high T levels).

62148493
>>62145262
>but also success was much more nepotism & regionally based 1-2 generations ago
The location part is a huge factor that very few people talk about nowadays. Back in the day there was very little chance to leave you area if you were poor and needed to help your parents around and no internet meant that all the wealth was built up on specific cities depending on the field of work instead of being spread around a bit like nowadays.
But really another huge factor is that most people in the past didn't try to get wealthy because of a combination of religion and not needing to, since life was so easy mode that you could live very comfortably unless you were dirt poor.
There was very little reason to try and make it past the average and, even though it was easier than now at the right places (in part because location gatekept workforce competition as mentioned before), it still required a lot of effort unless you were a nepo baby, so most just found a level where they were comfortable and chilled there.
Now the entire middle class is getting squeezed out, so you either make it or fall down, and people have become obsessed about monetary wealth to the point of mental illness because of this. The average zoomer would rather risk his current standard of living for a chance to make it into a higher class than remaining the same and watching how they get slowly pushed into a lower class than they started at.
This is why everyone is gambling on stocks or prediction markets and why people go en masse into proffessions like software engineering and law where the average is kinda mid but the top 10% make crazy money, even if they don't have any actual interest on those fields of work and chances are they will make the same money as any other educated job they could have liked more.
All generations are a product of their environment when growing up, and the way the current young gens are growing up is tailor made to turn them into sociopathic gamblers.

62148782
>>62144801
Income, and deriving an "income" is largely tied to business and their desire to hire people at a 1) livable wage and 2) not hire down or eliminate positions as they have for the last 30-40 years.

Having a job does not guarantee stability or success as corporations have continuously eroded the cost of labor by a) outsourcing b) offshoring c) making 1 person responsible for 5 peoples jobs d) importing foreign labor (h1b's etc) to erode wages even further. and e) union busting to ensure that nobody in the working class can rally for better pay, working conditions, stability, overtime pay etc...

basically, for the last 40 years large corporations (usually multi-nationals) have conspired together to erode wages and destroy the working class. who are now being displaced and replaced with foreign labor, automation and robots.

62148905
I have an unrelated pet peeve of mine. I'm sick and fucking tired of millennials larping as if it's still 2015 and they're paying off student loans on a one-store apartment.

I applaud the ones that understand that they're getting old. The delusion can't last forever. It's the ones that pretend they're still rocking the boat despite having a house and a savings account on the 6 digits.

62149041
>>62144801
poor people do not understand rich people's problems and rich people do not understand poor people's problems.

there is no solution. they will always be in conflict with each other.

rich people never have or have forgotten what its like to be poor so they repeat the same regurgitated bullshit about how poor people are stupid and lazy etc.
some of it is true but much of it is also out of touch. it definitely helps to have a network of other rich people for opportunities a poor person will just never encounter in their entire lives.
access to favorable loans etc.

on the opposite you have poor people who have a bunch of prejudice about how rich people got rich in the first place and what it takes to actually stay rich and a lot of that is also out of touch.

again, there is no solution.
in general, people are too full of themselves on both sides.

62149175
>>62148905
Nigger the only thing I have six figure of is dead and yes I am still paying student loans.

62149402
>>62148905
>dis nigga over here doesn't understand arrested development from 20 years of bait and switch careers
I'm not in that boat; I have a PhD and a stable career (that pays like half of what it should), but I get what my peers are going through. Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer. There's very few opportunities to bounce back if you get kneecapped with a layoff/accident/divorce/etc since its hard enough to get started with anything in the first place. Some of those people admittedly never tried, and they're losers; the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards

62150041
>>62144850
My jewish professor told me immigrants grow the economy tho

62150364
>>62149402
>Everything past 9/11 has been a downhill slide into a cratering society/civilization and constant economic downturn plastered over by the money printer.
I wouldn't say constant but yes.
>the ones that just got both barrels of reality to the face and can't muster the energy to start over I have some amount of sympathy towards
You lost me at "can't muster the energy to start over".

62150531
>>62150364
Tell me that again when you've spent 20 years pouring your life into something and have it fall out from underneath you. Its easier to paint yourself as a never gonna die hero before it happens to you. I've seen it mess some people up pretty hard

62150834
>>62150531
>Its easier to paint yourself as a never gonna die hero
I was thinking more of a normal person but obviously all you understand is the delusions of grandure the baby boomers raised you towards but could never meet.

62151920
>>62150834
All that made up headcanon is cool and whatever, but congratulations on never having been close to genuine hardship. I hope it stays that way for you

62153024
>>62148493
good post anon
so true

62153123
>>62148493
yep when I was a kid my parents moved us out from a somewhat sketchy area that is now very expensive. If we just stayed then they'd be far more wealthy on paper from the house alone. But in reality that's 30+ year hindsight and they simply valued safety and freedom rather than oh boy I hope my house goes up. Something I don't think our younger generations understand because of narcissism no desire for children. Everyone wants generational wealth but will have no generational legacy to even give it to

62153183
>>62149041
>poor people do not understand rich people's problems and rich people do not understand poor people's problems.
I think there's a cultural dimension too. White progressive types especially women, want to see uneducated white people as their lessors but brown people as simply victims of the system. Even objectively bad cultural aspects get excused and never addressed. So tyrone shoots andre over some trivial bullshit it's white people's fault. And when non-white people immigrate here after generations of being systematic exploiters ie Brahmins (like Kamala) these progressively minded ppl see absolutely nothing wrong there. But they despise Mr Bubba 4th gen redneck with alcoholic pregnant mom and dad who beat the shit out of him growing up

item.title: WTFWT — 35 replies — 26.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTFWT

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-04-24 18:48 UTC

OP

Yea, I’m thinking we’re back

Early replies (first 3)

62150553
Stop buying I get paid on Monday all I want is ONE more cheap buy
Please

62150563
>>62150529
Yeah I'm not falling for that KEK there's probably 6 million people who still have a stack who would gladly sell if they saw this thing go up to even a penny.

62150572
>>62150563
The thing is literally everyone who bought a million forgot about it a long long time ago. This will fly to $0.01 under the radar very fast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62150899
>>62150529
These threads always felt so saaarish.

62151006
>>62150529
Until it starts dropping zeros anybody shilling this is a confirmed jeetarino.

62151008
>>62150899
No, these threads feel like guerilla fighters laying low accumulating the best decentralized finance technology bar none. So many coins have become exactly what they once hated so they could integrate with tradfi.

62151013
>>62150899
yes lots of jeets push this coin but desu there's very few people even holding it at all.
I have a sui stack because i think we're gonna have a real alt run soon, but only like a $4k bag.
My honest thesis here is that when VET rugged, I put $2k in and made about $170k off of it in 2021. This coin is likely(imo) going to see some crazy shit like that, and then tank again for another 7 years. So I will 100% cash out at any reasonable gains but I have a good feeling about it.

62151017
>>62150529
I still hodl 1mil as a lotto ticket/novelty but I doubt it's ever going anywhere. This was easily the biggest letdown of the past cycle, it was a straight shot to $1, all they had to do was burn the excess. CZ could have easily helped send this shit to the moon but he decided to be a limpdick and leave it all to rot.

62151046
>>62151017
i honestly think its just because we haven't seen a real alt season since 2021. we may see one very soon.

62151093
>>62151046
It legit feels over this time. Even 2022 didn't feel this shit, there was still a feeling that stuff would come back. 2024/25 came and went with barely any real movement. BTC did a pathetic 2x and then dumped. I think most of the liquidity and investment that would have gone to crypto just went to AI instead, as well as to cash positions because of the endless FUD that happens every other week.

62151134
>>62151093
it can't be over. crypto is a new asset class entirely, and with the rate that its being positioned in real-world usage there would have to be a societal collapse to see it all come down to zero. Of course you never know, but i don't think that it's over yet.

62151136
>>62151093
crypto is now like horse betting. some of these crypto blockchain/oracle technologies will become integrated with future platforms. some tokens will have a meaningful role down the line and thus become mainstream.
not gonna sugar coat it, capital has moved on to other pastures AI, quantum, hell even trading cards ate cryptos lunch lol.
this fate is well-deserved for 2 reasons. the crypto speculators front-ran the profits too vigorously and demolished any semblance of expected value. also most people have matured though pain and realized that tangible assets are better than total loss

62151357
how fucking delusional are you retards?

62151711
>>62151093
>It legit feels over this time. Even 2022 didn't feel this shit
That’s exactly how I feel. No other “bear season” felt like this. There is literally no hope left. BTC and eth both hit ATHs while everything else was in the gutter. Both could do another 2x and alts would still be down 80%. The hype and liquidity is gone.

62151816
>>62151357
As i said, saaarish.

62151875
>>62151357
Who cares what the chart looks like? Lunc could do a 1000x and it would still look like a flat line.

62151914
shorted and took profits, what gives?

62152679
I’m so depressed. I don’t even know what stage of depression I’m in but I don’t feel anything anymore. I can’t cry even if I wanted to. The good thoughts that used to make me feel happy don’t do anything anymore. I just want to find a nice quiet place in the mountains away from everyone else and rot away peacefully. It’s not like I’m contributing anything to society anyways

62152731
>>62150563
A penny? I'm waiting for $1. Stay poor.

62152739
>>62150572
Yup. Literally the next doge.

62152974
>>62151875
with that logic just go put it all on red

62153028
>>62152974
Why are you such a whiny little faggot?

62153035
>>62153028
>says the whiny faggot

62153071
>>62153035
you chose to enter the LUNC thread where loyalists gather. Let me remind you of all the shilled coins that people promoted here and where are they now?
LUNC (currently 300m mkt cap) will laugh on the gravestones of bat, rose, iexec, dogebonk, shib, hoge, and kenshi.

62153091
>>62151357
I hold LUNC because it would be so based to tell people I made it because I bought that chart.

62153094
>>62153071
I chose to enter thats correct. and i chose to voice my opinion. calling me whiner while whining about me stating an opinion. and you have to deal with it.

62153101
>>62153071
So if LUNC has a growing market cap, shrinking supply, and valid tech, then from a risk/reward perspective, it's riskier to ignore LUNC at these prices than it is to buy.
You wont find that risk/reward asymmetry playing roulette.

62153175
Lunc
Will
Burn
90%
Of
Token
We
Will
Go
Straight
To
$100

62153192
>>62152974
There’s a higher chance of making money on lunc compared to most of the shit out there.

62153204
>>62150563
holy shit my 1m wrapped lunas just unwrapped and coinbase HAS to pay me $1 each

62153319
>>62153204
Based for not falling the Coinbase psyop and not moving it off the exchange. WLUNA is so valuable you cannot even buy it anymore

62153446
Seeing lot of newfags here who dont know what is coming.
$0.13 is foretold is multiple schizos on biz and youtubes dreams. So load up enough for .13 to make you at least 1mm after taxes. In burger land where I'm at youll need at least 13-14mm LUNC

62153463
>>62153446
Hard to believe there are REAL people on this board who saw lunc at sub 150m mcap and thought
>nahh I won't stack here
At .01 I'm a millionaire
At .13 I'm retiring
When USTC repegs and MIR reactivates I'll be a king
If there's any newfags in here buy a bag of ATOM (16% APR staked)
When LUNA moons cosmos network will go crazy too

item.title: >ai stocks climbing 20-30% every fucking day >btc crabs >alts go to 0 WHY THE FUCK AM I HOLDING THIS SHIT — 18 replies — 19.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>ai stocks climbing 20-30% every fucking day >btc crabs >alts go to 0 WHY THE FUCK AM I HOLDING THIS SHIT

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-04-24 19:12 UTC

OP

>ai stocks climbing 20-30% every fucking day
>btc crabs
>alts go to 0
WHY THE FUCK AM I HOLDING THIS SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

62152530
>>62152524
its been said for a long time: chips > btc

62152565
>>62152524
I'd recommend learning the Bitcoin 4 year cycle.
Everybody knew we'd have a shitty year except for people in denial. Things dumped and will crab until they get good again around October or so.

62152582
>>62152565
yeah learn about astrology too

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62152600
>>62152582
Are you trying to say the 4 year cycle is bullshit, or are you saying astrology is legitimate?

62152601
>>62152524
i fucking sold my $30 INTC calls November of last year for 13 bucks. 4x'ed my money and was happy about it. today those same calls are trading at $54.....

many such cases

62152608
>>62152524
You could always fomo in, might work out or might not

62152613
>>62152524
shoulda sold when i did. When i realised the entire crypto market was held up by idiots, criminals and wannabe criminals and con artists. Sam Bankman-kike Fried was the slap in the face i needed.

>a lazy, retarded kike who didn't even bother covering his tracks, just trying to bribe his way out of any problems, managed to get his scam-market platform to the number 3 position on earth, with billions in crypto on it.

I was a fool to invest at all, i should have bought stocks.

62152626
>>62152524
Bulltrap.
It will fall soon enough. 60+%
Then I feast.

62152635
>>62152524
yeah look at this AI stock too

62152638
>>62152626
sure, but will it 4x again before it does that?

62152644
>>62152524
>>62152635
another AI stock up big time

62152651
>>62152524
>>62152635
>>62152644
why do people hate AI stocks?

62152656
>>62152638
Nah. Not enough money around.

62152693
>>62152600
>4 year cycle
It will be 4 years since the end of the last cycle on May 22nd. The bull market hasn't even started yet. You have one month left to accumulate faggot

62152769
>>62152635
>cleveland-cliff
theyre set up for dumping a steamer

62152928
>>62152693
It always reaches an ATH 12-18mo after a halfing followed by a bear market. That is exactly what happened.
The bull was 2024-2025. You missed it.

62153052
>>62152524
Not pure AI, though. Albeit driven by AI. Guess investors got sick of not becoming billionaires just from holding memory stocks. Correction. those are getting pumped again these days. Good thing I held. Maybe those who didn't get in on Intel earlier.

62153601
>>62152600
lmao how stupid are you?

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 47 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-04-24 16:42 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144501
>>62144289
Only people who believe that their ancestors were shat out of a monkeys ass believe there is no meaning to this life.

62144519
>>62144501
Lol ok kid let's see if you gonna say that on your deathbed or if you get cancer. Retarded NPCs like you will never be saved

62144572
>>62144475
>top 10%
>made it

kek

62144576
>>62143145
a monk would live like this but without the faggot TV and gaming setup
that's what bothers me about these "Let me guess. You need more?" images, they're not based and ascetic if you have a faggot TV

62144982
>>62144519
Wtf is that even supposed to mean?

62144995
>>62144982
There's a few active anons on the board that just try to be as caustic and derisive towards any topic or poster as possible. There's at least 2 itt

62144996
>>62144982
It means you are a retarded brownoid faggot basedboy cuck. Kill yourself

62145001
>>62144995
>>62144996
Boom right on cue
They are generally pretty quick to reply as well because that kind of derangement generally stems from being terminally online

62145005
I've read
>how to win friends and influence people
>art of war
>12 rules by peterson
>48 laws of power
>atomic habits
>the art of not giving a fuck
>the art of the deal
>meditations of auruelius(stoic)
>4 hour work week
>the secret
>rich dad poor dad and the sequel

And yet my life still sucks. And before you say "but anon your'e supposed to do the things the books say to do not just read them!" well I have. For example richdad poordad says to borrow money from your friends and buy a house, flip it and then give them back their money-the exact amount they let you borrow. Well I asked all my friends for money and NONE of them gave me any. Only one even asked what it was for and when I told him he laughed in my face and said "You have to know how to flip a house to flip a house."

What the fuck

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432
Izzat farming thread
Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

62148909
>>62144289
this

62151619
>>62144395
... if you want to end up in hell

62151984
>>62144435
Marcus Aurelius didn't trust the slut, he knew she was bad when he married her. Thing was, she was the daughter of the previous emperor who was a cool guy and Aurelius married her out of loyalty to him (plus he got to be emperor himself out of it).

62152046
>>62143054
for me its several different stimulants and an ssri
amazingly im still a husk of a human, its honestly impressive financially speaking

62152662
Scam

62152741
>>62143054
Many of those things have improved my life a lot. Of course, I am not a billionaire but I like to enjoy the little things in life

62152905
>>62145005
You're supposed to read good fiction and let your mind wander, not that (((self help))) slop.
